name: Enterprise Workflows Knowledge Pack — Source Registry
policy: Sources are REGISTERED REFERENCES compiled from general professional and public-domain knowledge for governance and attribution. They are NOT freshly retrieved documents: retrievedUtc is null and exact URLs/text must be independently verified before any citation or use. The pack contains only ORIGINAL summaries — no verbatim copying of any source, vendor schema, vendor UI, or protected standard text. verbatimCopyAllowed is false for every protected/official source. Conceptual frameworks are registered as topic families, not specific documents; no clause numbers, titles, or authors are asserted.

sources.title: BPMN business-process modelling notation concepts (process, task, gateway, lane, event) — public concept family
sources.publisher: Object Management Group (OMG) — concept attribution only
sources.licenseNote: BPMN is a published notation; only widely-known concepts (tasks, gateways, swimlanes, events) are summarised. No specification text is reproduced.
sources.allowedUse: Original conceptual summary of process-modelling vocabulary only.
sources.verificationStatus: Concept family registered from general knowledge; verify against the current BPMN specification before formal citation.
sources.limitationNote: Conceptual modelling vocabulary only. Not a BPMN conformance claim and not the specification text.

sources.title: IT service-management lifecycle concepts (incident, change, request, SLA, escalation) — public concept family
sources.publisher: ITSM practice community (ITIL concept lineage) — concept attribution only
sources.licenseNote: ITIL publications are protected; only widely-known process concepts are summarised. No publication text is reproduced.
sources.allowedUse: Original conceptual summary of incident/change/request/SLA concepts only.
sources.verificationStatus: Concept family registered; verify against current licensed ITIL guidance before formal citation.
sources.limitationNote: Practical ITSM concept summary; does not replace the licensed ITIL guidance or imply certification.

sources.title: Banking maker/checker and four-eyes dual-control concepts — public banking-control concept family
sources.publisher: General banking-operations practice — concept attribution only
sources.licenseNote: Maker/checker and four-eyes are widely-known control concepts; no proprietary procedure manual is reproduced.
sources.allowedUse: Original conceptual summary of segregation-of-duties controls only.
sources.verificationStatus: Concept family registered; institution-specific procedures must be verified against internal policy.
sources.limitationNote: Control-concept summary only. Not a control attestation or institution policy.

sources.title: FATF — AML/CFT Recommendations and risk-based approach concepts (family)
sources.licenseNote: © FATF/OECD. Public guidance; attribution required, no misrepresentation, no verbatim redistribution implied.
sources.allowedUse: Original summary of AML/CFT and KYC/CDD/EDD principles only.
sources.verificationStatus: Reference registered; verify current Recommendations text before citing.
sources.limitationNote: AML/CFT awareness only; apply local transposition and current law. Not legal advice.

sources.title: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BIS) — operational-risk and internal-control principles (family)
sources.publisher: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision / Bank for International Settlements
sources.licenseNote: © BIS. Public material; attribution required, no verbatim redistribution implied.
sources.allowedUse: Original summary of operational-risk and control principles only.
sources.verificationStatus: Reference registered; verify current text before citing.
sources.limitationNote: Principle-level awareness only. National implementation varies; not regulatory advice.

sources.title: ISO/IEC management-system and information-security control concepts (e.g., access control, change control, records) — PROTECTED
sources.licenseNote: © ISO. Protected, paid standard text; verbatim copying prohibited without licence.
sources.allowedUse: Original control-style summary of widely-known principles only.
sources.verificationStatus: Reference registered; clause numbers/wording require verification against the licensed standards.
sources.limitationNote: Practical control-style summary; does not replace the licensed ISO standard or imply certification.

sources.title: NIST — public risk-management, contingency (backup/restore) and audit-logging guidance (family)
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (US)
sources.licenseNote: US Government work — generally public domain; verify per publication.
sources.allowedUse: Original summary; public-domain status to be confirmed per document.
sources.verificationStatus: Reference registered; verify current publication before citing.
sources.limitationNote: Security/risk/continuity awareness only; not a control certification.

sources.title: OWASP — access-control, audit-logging and input-validation secure-coding guidance (family)
sources.licenseNote: OWASP content is typically open-licensed (e.g., CC); confirm licence per page.
sources.allowedUse: Original summary; open-licence terms to be confirmed per page.
sources.verificationStatus: Reference registered; verify current guidance before citing.
sources.limitationNote: Secure-coding awareness only (authorization, audit, validation); not a security assurance.

sources.title: Microsoft .NET / ASP.NET Core / EF Core official documentation (transactions, concurrency tokens, authorization) (family)
sources.licenseNote: © Microsoft. Docs under Microsoft terms; verbatim reuse limited.
sources.allowedUse: Original summary of documented behaviour only.
sources.verificationStatus: Reference registered; verify against current docs for the targeted version.
sources.limitationNote: Behaviour can change between framework versions; verify for the deployed version. Used for the workflow code-generation standard.

sources.title: Payment-message and rejection/return reason-code concepts (ISO 20022-style families, scheme return codes) — public concept family
sources.licenseNote: Scheme rulebooks and the ISO 20022 dictionary are protected; only the generic notion of mapped, retryable/non-retryable reason codes is summarised. No code list or rulebook text is reproduced.
sources.allowedUse: Original conceptual summary of rejection/return-code handling only.
sources.verificationStatus: Concept family registered; specific code values and scheme rules must be verified against the applicable rulebook before use.
sources.limitationNote: Not a code list and not scheme-rule guidance. Specific rejection codes are illustrative only.

sources.title: LocalAIFactory internal workflow and governance practice (approval lifecycle, human-approval-before-AI-code-change, knowledge import/approval)
sources.licenseNote: Original internal summary authored for LocalAIFactory.
sources.verificationStatus: Internal note; reflects LocalAIFactory operating guidance (CLAUDE.md / MASTER_VISION.md) as of the review date.
sources.limitationNote: Internal practice only; not a regulatory control or external standard. Workflow fixtures are synthetic prototypes.
